summary: Tested Analytics page (/analytics) end-to-end: 16/16 backend pytest tests pass (all KPI/map/export/weekly/daily/heatmap/activities/monthly/month endpoints). Frontend renders KPIs, Leaflet heat map with 20 amber markers (CARTO dark tiles), all Recharts charts, top destinos, month cards, filter panel, and 3 export buttons. CSV/XLSX/PDF downloads triggered successfully. Found ONE real bug: 'Limpar' button does not actually reset KPIs (stale-closure issue in Analytics.jsx).

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking 'Limpar' (data-testid=clear-filters) does NOT reload data with cleared filter. After applying unidade='UPA SANTO AMARO' (KPI dropped 1568->443 km), clicking Limpar still shows 443 (should restore to 1568). The state (start/end/unidade) is cleared in the UI, but the subsequent API call still uses the previous filter values.

issues: Visual layout is premium and polished: dark theme + amber #F59E0B, terminal style, KPI cards aligned, CARTO dark tiles + amber heat layer + amber markers render correctly, charts readable, header shows '20 unidades · 53 visitas'. No design issues.

action_items: Fix stale-closure bug in /app/frontend/src/pages/Analytics.jsx line 204: `<button onClick={() => { clearFilters(); setTimeout(loadAll, 50); }} data-testid="clear-filters">`. The setTimeout captures the OLD loadAll reference (bound via useCallback to the OLD filterQS, which closes over old start/end/unidade). The 50ms delay is not enough for React to re-render and recreate loadAll. Suggested fix: use a reload-trigger pattern, e.g. `const [reloadTick, setReloadTick] = useState(0); useEffect(() => { loadAll(); }, [reloadTick, loadAll]); const clearFilters = () => { setStart(''); setEnd(''); setUnidade(''); setReloadTick(t => t+1); };` — or alternatively pass explicit empty values to a non-closure-bound fetcher.
critical_code_review_comments: Analytics.jsx file is 506 lines — borderline; not urgent but consider extracting <KpiGrid/>, <HeatmapMap/>, <FilterPanel/>, <MonthlyChart/> sub-components.
Stale-closure pitfall: relying on setTimeout to wait for React state — fragile. Prefer effect-based or arg-based fetchers (see action_items).
Promise.allSettled used correctly so partial failures don't crash the page — good resilience.
Map fetch is decoupled from KPIs which keeps perceived perf high — good UX.
All key elements expose data-testid attributes — excellent testability.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_analytics.py (added 7 tests: test_daily, test_heatmap_weekday, test_activities, test_monthly_12m, test_month_detail, test_kpis_filters_full, test_export_content_disposition)
seed_data_creation: Nenhum. Usou o banco_de_dados.json existente (267 rotas; 20 unidades, 53 visitas; geocode cache 20/20 resolvidos).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend Analytics totalmente funcional. Frontend tem 1 bug claro de stale-closure no botão Limpar — fix sugerido em action_items. Map heat + markers, CSV/XLSX/PDF downloads, filtros (Aplicar), month modal — todos funcionam. Para re-testar Limpar: abrir /analytics, aguardar kpi-total-km mostrar '1568', selecionar filter-unidade -> 'UPA SANTO AMARO', clicar apply-filters (vira 443), clicar clear-filters, aguardar 2s, conferir kpi-total-km voltou a 1568.
rca_of_the_issue: RCA do bug Limpar: Analytics.jsx:204 onClick={() => { clearFilters(); setTimeout(loadAll, 50); }}. clearFilters() faz setStart(''), setEnd(''), setUnidade('') — async, agendam re-render. setTimeout(loadAll, 50) captura a referência CURRENT de loadAll, que é uma useCallback bound a filterQS, que por sua vez fecha sobre as variáveis start/end/unidade do render anterior (ainda com unidade='UPA SANTO AMARO'). Após 50ms, mesmo que React tenha re-renderizado, a closure não atualizou — o fetch usa o filtro antigo. Reprodução: ver context_for_next_testing_agent. Mitigação proposta em action_items.
